title: gastrolobium sericeum
intro: Gastrolobium sericeum, also known as the sticky pea or woolly bitter pea, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family that is native to Western Australia. The medicinal properties of Gastrolobium sericeum include its potential to exhibit antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant activities. The plant is believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Gastrolobium sericeum may be made by drying the leaves, stems, or roots, and using them in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
